Name: A24
Notes: Founded 2012 by Daniel Katz, David Fenkel, John Hodges. Oscar-winning indie studio (Moonlight, Everything Everywhere). Co-finances and acquires. Known for auteur-driven content.

Name: Endeavor Content
Notes: WME parent company's content arm. Finances, packages, and sells film/TV globally. Sold majority stake to Korea's CJ ENM in 2022.

Name: Blumhouse Productions
Notes: Founded by Jason Blum. Low-budget horror/thriller model. Get Out, Paranormal Activity, The Purge. Micro-budget model ($3-5M typical). First-look deal with Universal.

Name: Media Rights Capital (MRC)
Notes: Founded by Modi Wiczyk and Asif Satchu. House of Cards, Knives Out, Ted Lasso. Full-service studio financing and production.

Name: Participant Media
Notes: Founded by Jeff Skoll (eBay). Social-impact focused. An Inconvenient Truth, Spotlight, Green Book. Finances films with social relevance.

Name: Skydance Media
Notes: Founded by David Ellison (Larry Ellison's son). Mission Impossible, Top Gun: Maverick, The Old Guard. Major co-financing partner. Merging with Paramount.

Name: Legendary Entertainment
Notes: Owned by Wanda Group (China). Dune, Godzilla vs Kong, The Dark Knight trilogy. Major tentpole financing. Co-finances with Warner Bros and Universal.

Name: STX Entertainment
Notes: Founded by Robert Simonds. Bad Moms, Hustlers. Mid-budget commercial films. Filed bankruptcy 2023, restructured as ErosSTX.

Name: Annapurna Pictures
Notes: Founded by Megan Ellison. Her, American Hustle, Booksmart. Prestige and auteur-driven content. Also in gaming and TV.

Name: Lionsgate
Notes: Publicly traded (LGF). Hunger Games, John Wick, Saw. Vertically integrated mini-major. Starz subsidiary. Invests across budget ranges.

Name: NEON
Notes: Founded by Tom Quinn and Tim League. Parasite, I Tonya, Anatomy of a Fall. Acquires and co-finances. Growing into production.

Name: Magnolia Pictures
Notes: Founded by Eamonn Bowles. Part of 2929 Entertainment (Mark Cuban/Todd Wagner). Indie distribution and acquisition. Also Magnet Releasing.

Name: IFC Films
Notes: AMC Networks subsidiary. Boyhood, My Big Fat Greek Wedding. Theatrical and day-and-date releasing. Acquires at festivals.

Name: Killer Films
Notes: Founded by Christine Vachon and Pamela Koffler. Far From Heaven, Carol, Still Alice. Pioneering indie producer. Co-finances with equity and pre-sales.

Name: Plan B Entertainment
Notes: Brad Pitt's production company. 12 Years a Slave, Moonlight, The Big Short. Academy Award-winning productions. First-look deal with Warner Bros.

Name: Chernin Entertainment
Notes: Founded by Peter Chernin (former News Corp president). Planet of the Apes trilogy, Ford v Ferrari, Hidden Figures. Also Chernin Group invests in media/tech.

Name: Imagine Entertainment
Notes: Founded by Brian Grazer and Ron Howard. A Beautiful Mind, Apollo 13, Arrested Development. Major studio relationships across film and TV.

Name: Bad Robot Productions
Notes: Founded by J.J. Abrams. Star Wars: The Force Awakens, Mission Impossible III, Westworld. Exclusive deal with Warner Bros Discovery (reported $250M).

Name: Harpo Films
Location: Chicago IL / Los Angeles CA USA
Notes: Oprah Winfrey's production company. Beloved, The Color Purple (2023), Oprah's Book Club adaptations. Focus on uplifting stories, diverse voices.

Name: Tyler Perry Studios
Notes: Tyler Perry's vertically integrated studio on 330-acre lot in Atlanta. Madea franchise. Produces film, TV, and stage. Owns distribution pipeline. BET+ deal.

Name: MACRO
Notes: Founded by Charles D. King. Fences, Mudbound, Judas and the Black Messiah. Focus on diverse storytelling. Also MACRO Television Studios.

Name: Higher Ground Productions
Location: Washington DC / Los Angeles CA USA
Notes: Founded by Barack and Michelle Obama. American Factory, Crip Camp, Leave the World Behind. Multi-year Netflix deal. Documentary and narrative.

Name: Monkeypaw Productions
Notes: Founded by Jordan Peele. Get Out, Us, Nope. Universal first-look deal. Horror and social thriller focus.

Name: ARRAY
Notes: Founded by Ava DuVernay. Selma, When They See Us, 13th. Distribution collective for independent films by people of color and women. ARRAY Releasing distributes.

Name: 30West
Notes: Founded by Micah Green and Dan Friedkin. Co-finances and sells. Killers of the Flower Moon, Uncut Gems, Spencer. Elite film finance and sales advisory.

Name: CAA Media Finance
Notes: Creative Artists Agency's film finance division. Packages and finances films using agency talent. One of the biggest packagers in Hollywood.

Name: UTA Independent Film Group
Notes: United Talent Agency's indie film finance arm. Packages and co-finances independent films. Festival acquisitions and presales.

Name: WME Independent
Notes: William Morris Endeavor's independent film division. Packaging, financing, and sales for independent films.

Name: Bankside Films
Notes: International sales and co-financing. Room, Lady Macbeth. UK-based sales agent that co-finances select titles.

Name: FilmNation Entertainment
Notes: Founded by Glen Basner. Arrival, The Big Sick. Major indie film finance, production, and international sales company.

Name: HanWay Films
Notes: International sales agent and co-financier. Shame, In Bruges. Established UK-based sales company.

Name: Rocket Science
Notes: International sales and financing. London-based. Sells worldwide rights and co-finances independent features.

Name: Myriad Pictures
Notes: International sales and production. Founded by Kirk D'Amico. Finances and sells independent films worldwide.

Name: XYZ Films
Notes: Genre-focused production and sales. The Raid, Mandy. Specializes in action, horror, sci-fi. Also talent management.

Name: Protagonist Pictures
Notes: International sales and co-production. Ex Machina. UK-based. Festival-driven acquisitions and equity co-financing.

Name: The Exchange
Notes: International sales company. Based in LA. Pre-sells international territories to finance independent productions.

Name: Voltage Pictures
Notes: International sales and production. Dallas Buyers Club, The Hurt Locker. Active in presales-based financing.

Name: AGC Studios
Notes: Founded by Stuart Ford (former IM Global). Finances, produces, and sells. Major independent studio with slate financing.

Name: Anton Capital Entertainment
Notes: Founded by Sébastien Raybaud. Co-finances studio films. Terminator: Dark Fate, Where the Crawdads Sing. European co-financing partner.

Name: TPG Capital (Entertainment)
Location: San Francisco CA / Fort Worth TX USA
Notes: Major PE firm with media/entertainment division. Invested in CAA, DirecTV Entertainment, entertainment platforms. AUM $135B+.

Name: Apollo Global Management (Media)
Notes: Alternative investment firm. Yahoo (Verizon Media), Cox Media, entertainment debt financing. AUM $600B+.

Name: Ares Management (Media)
Notes: Alternative investment manager with media/entertainment credit and equity investments. AUM $395B+. LA-based, close to entertainment industry.

Name: Eldridge Industries
Notes: Founded by Todd Boehly. Dick Clark Productions, MRC (partial owner), Security Benefit, Chelsea FC. Major media/entertainment investor.

Name: RedBird Capital Partners
Notes: Founded by Gerry Cardinale (ex-Goldman Sachs). YES Network, Skydance (investor), On Location. Sports and entertainment focus.

Name: Shamrock Capital
Notes: Disney family office (Roy E. Disney legacy). Focuses on media, entertainment, and communications. Music rights, content libraries, media companies.

Name: Stripes Group
Notes: Growth equity firm with media investments. Invested in media/consumer brands. Founded by Ken Fox.

Name: Raine Group
Notes: Merchant bank focused on TMT (Technology, Media, Telecom). Advised on major media M&A. Investors in entertainment companies.

Name: Guggenheim Partners (Entertainment)
Location: New York / Chicago / Los Angeles USA
Notes: Investment and advisory firm. Former owners of Dick Clark Productions. Active in entertainment/media investments. AUM $218B+.

Name: Rizvi Traverse Management
Location: Los Angeles CA / New York NY USA
Notes: Founded by Suhail Rizvi. Early Twitter investor. ICM Partners (former investor), Playboy, entertainment tech. Private, low-profile.

Name: Providence Equity Partners
Notes: Major PE firm focused on media/communications/education. Hulu (early investor), Univision, Yankees Entertainment. AUM $50B+.

Name: Evolution Media Capital
Notes: Entertainment-focused merchant bank. TPG Growth partner. Invests in and advises media/entertainment companies. Hello Sunshine sale advisor.

Name: Candle Media (Hello Sunshine)
Notes: Reese Witherspoon's Hello Sunshine merged into Candle Media (Blackstone-backed). Kevin Mayer and Tom Staggs. Content-driven media company. Big Little Lies.

Name: Maximum Effort
Notes: Ryan Reynolds' production company and marketing firm. Free Guy, Deadpool marketing. Now part of MNTN. Content and brand entertainment.

Name: SpringHill Company
Notes: LeBron James and Maverick Carter. Space Jam: A New Legacy, The Shop. Content, brand, consumer products. RedBird Capital invested $725M valuation.

Name: Green Door Pictures
Notes: Idris Elba's production company. Luther, Concrete Cowboy. Film and TV production focused on diverse stories.

Name: Tribeca Enterprises
Notes: Robert De Niro and Jane Rosenthal. Tribeca Film Festival, Tribeca Studios. Content production, festival, and distribution platform.

Name: Smokehouse Pictures
Notes: George Clooney and Grant Heslov. Argo, The Midnight Sky, The Boys in the Boat. Prestige drama focus. Warner Bros relationship.

Name: LuckyChap Entertainment
Notes: Margot Robbie, Tom Ackerley, Josey McNamara. Barbie, I Tonya, Promising Young Woman. Major commercial and prestige hits.

Name: Outlier Society
Notes: Michael B. Jordan's production company. Creed III, A Journal for Jordan. First-look deal with Amazon. Inclusion rider pioneer.

Name: Hoorae Media
Notes: Issa Rae's media company. Insecure, Rap Sh!t. Includes Hoorae Films, ColorCreative management, Raedio audio. WarnerMedia deal.

Name: Get Lifted Film Co
Notes: John Legend and Mike Jackson. La La Land, Jingle Jangle, Underground. Film and TV production. Grammy/Oscar/Emmy/Tony winner.

Name: Hillbilly Films
Notes: Emma Thompson's production company. Based in UK. Focus on literary adaptations and female-driven stories.

Name: SunnyMarch
Notes: Benedict Cumberbatch and Adam Ackland. The Mauritanian, Eric (Netflix). UK-based. Focus on socially relevant drama.

Name: Simpson Street
Notes: Kerry Washington's production company. Scandal producer. Focuses on stories centered on women and people of color. ABC deal.

Name: MountainA Films
Notes: Natalie Portman's production company. Co-founded with Sophie Mas. Lady in the Lake, A Tale of Love and Darkness.

Name: Catapult Film Fund
Notes: Founded by Nion McEvoy. Provides seed funding grants for documentaries in early development. Has funded 500+ films.

Name: Sundance Documentary Fund
Notes: Part of Sundance Institute. Grants for documentary films. Multiple grant categories. Highly competitive. Open call submissions.

Name: Fork Films
Notes: Founded by Abigail Disney. Supports documentary films with social justice focus. Also produces. The Armor of Light.

Name: Just Films (Ford Foundation)
Notes: Ford Foundation's film funding initiative. Supports nonfiction storytelling for social justice. Major documentary funder.

Name: Influence Film Foundation
Notes: Supports distribution and impact of documentary films. Provides resources and partnerships for impact campaigns.

Name: Doc Society (formerly BRITDOC)
Location: London UK / New York NY USA
Notes: Formerly BRITDOC Foundation. Supports documentary filmmakers globally. Good Pitch, climate story fund. Major doc ecosystem builder.

Name: Chicken & Egg Pictures
Notes: Supports women and non-binary documentary filmmakers. (Egg)celerator Lab. Flexible funding and mentorship.

Name: ITVS (Independent Television Service)
Notes: Funds and co-produces independent documentaries and dramas for public television. Funded 1500+ films. Open Call and Diversity Fund.

Name: POV / PBS
Notes: America's longest-running documentary series (PBS). Acquires and broadcasts independent docs. Also POV Shorts.

Name: Impact Partners
Notes: Founded by Dan Cogan and Jenny Raskin. Icarus, RBG, The Cove. Equity investment in documentary films for social impact.

Name: Film4 (Channel 4)
Notes: Channel 4's film division. 12 Years a Slave, Three Billboards, Room. Development and co-production funding. UK filmmakers focus.

Name: BBC Films
Notes: BBC's theatrical film arm. Philomena, Brooklyn, Rocks. Co-finances British films. Annual slate of 8-12 films.

Name: BFI (British Film Institute)
Notes: UK's lead body for film. Distributes National Lottery funding. BFI Film Fund finances British films. Annual budget ~£27M.

Name: Working Title Films
Notes: Eric Fellner and Tim Bevan. Darkest Hour, Les Misérables, Bridget Jones. Universal Pictures subsidiary. Major UK-based production.

Name: StudioCanal
Notes: European mini-major. Vivendi subsidiary. Paddington, Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy. Produces, acquires, and distributes across Europe.

Name: See-Saw Films
Notes: Iain Canning and Emile Sherman. The King's Speech, Lion, The Power of the Dog. UK/Australia production. Prestige focus.

Name: DNA Films
Notes: Andrew Macdonald and Allon Reich. 28 Days Later, Ex Machina, Annihilation. Fox Searchlight deal. Genre and prestige.

Name: Heyday Films
Notes: David Heyman. Harry Potter franchise, Gravity, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, Barbie. One of UK's most prolific producers. Warner Bros relationship.

Name: Element Pictures
Notes: Ed Guiney and Andrew Lowe. The Favourite, Room, Normal People, The Banshees of Inisherin. Leading Irish production company.

Name: Curzon
Notes: Curzon Artificial Eye distribution + Curzon Cinemas chain. Acquires and distributes arthouse/independent films in UK. Also operates Curzon Home Cinema.

Name: Pathé
Notes: Major European film company. Co-finances and distributes. The Queen, Selma (European rights). Production and cinema chain.

Name: Wild Bunch International
Notes: Major European sales agent and co-financier. Founded by Vincent Maraval. European arthouse and genre focus.

Name: MK2 Films
Notes: French distributor, sales agent, and cinema operator. Curates arthouse cinema. Also MK2 cinemas chain.

Name: UK Film Tax Relief (HMRC)
Notes: UK tax relief for qualifying British films. 25% Audio Visual Expenditure Credit (from 2024). Must pass cultural test or qualify as official co-production.

Name: Georgia Film Tax Credit
Notes: 30% base credit (20% + 10% Georgia logo). Minimum $500K spend in GA. Transferable credits. Major hub: Walking Dead, Black Panther, Avengers: Endgame.

Name: New Mexico Film Incentive
Notes: 25% base (30% for TV pilots, 35% for qualifying rural areas). Refundable. Breaking Bad, Oppenheimer, Sicario. Netflix studio in ABQ.

Name: Louisiana Film Tax Credit
Notes: 25% base + 15% for Louisiana resident labor. Transferable. Minimum $300K spend. 12 Years a Slave, Beasts of the Southern Wild.

Name: British Columbia Film Credit
Notes: 28% basic FIBC + 6% regional bonus + 16% DAVE digital animation. Vancouver is 'Hollywood North'. Deadpool, Planet of the Apes.

Name: Australia Producer Offset
Notes: 40% Producer Offset for qualifying Australian feature films. 20% for other formats. Also 16.5% Location Offset for international productions.

Name: Ireland Section 481
Notes: 32% on eligible Irish expenditure. Star Wars: The Last Jedi, Normal People. Strong creative talent base. Also Screen Ireland development funding.

Name: Hungary Film Incentive
Notes: 30% cash rebate. Blade Runner 2049, The Martian, Dune. Competitive crew rates. Origo Studios, Korda Studios.

Name: Czech Republic Film Incentive
Notes: 20% cash rebate on Czech spend. Additional 10% for VFX. Spider-Man: Far From Home, The Gray Man. Barrandov Studios.

Name: South Korea Film Incentive
Notes: Up to 30% for foreign productions. Korean content wave driving investment. Squid Game location incentives.

Name: Cross Creek Pictures
Notes: Founded by Timmy Thompson and Tyler Thompson. Black Swan, Hacksaw Ridge, Everest. Equity co-financing with major studios.

Name: Sierra/Affinity (now part of AGC)
Notes: International sales and financing. Now part of AGC Studios. Previously financed and sold major independent films.

Name: Black Bear Pictures
Notes: Founded by Teddy Schwarzman. The Imitation Game, The Banker, Passing. Equity-finances and produces prestige films.

Name: Waypoint Entertainment
Notes: Ken Kao's production company. La La Land (co-financed), The Neon Demon. Art-house and prestige focus.

Name: Ingenious Media
Notes: Patrick McKenna's UK film finance company. One of the largest independent film financiers in Europe. Avatar, Life of Pi (co-financed). EIS/SEIS structures.

Name: Great Point Media
Location: London UK / New York NY USA
Notes: Robert Halmi Jr. and Jim Reeve. Film and TV finance. Produces and invests. Great Point Capital provides equity.

Name: Cornerstone Films
Notes: International sales and co-financing. Alison Thompson and Mark Gooder. The Father, Benediction. Prestige sales.

Name: Embankment Films
Notes: International sales and production. Tim Haslam and Hugo Grumbar. UK-based. Co-finances and sells globally.

Name: Independent Film & Television Alliance (IFTA)
Notes: Global trade association for independent film/TV. Organizes American Film Market (AFM). Key networking for film finance.

Name: Endeavor Content Capital
Notes: Debt and equity financing division of Endeavor Content. Provides production financing and gap financing.

Name: William Morris Endeavor (WME)
Notes: Major talent agency. Packages films and TV shows. WME Independent handles indie film finance and sales.

Name: Topic Studios
Notes: First Look Media's entertainment studio (Pierre Omidyar). Spotlight on social issues. Produces film and TV. Won Oscar for Spotlight.

Name: Concordia Studio
Notes: Social impact entertainment studio. Backed by Emerson Collective (Laurene Powell Jobs). Produces film/TV with social impact lens.

Name: Elevated Films
Notes: Cassian Elwes-founded sales and finance company. Specializes in independent film financing and festival sales.

Name: WestEnd Films
Notes: International sales and co-financing. Maya Amsellem. UK-based. Art-house and commercial independent films.

Name: Cinetic Media
Notes: John Sloss' advisory and sales company. Festival strategy, financing, and deal-making. Boyhood, Super Size Me.

Name: Endeavor Capital
Notes: Parent company of WME. Silver Lake invested $4B+. Entertainment conglomerate: agency, events (UFC), content financing.

Name: Bron Studios
Notes: Founded by Aaron L. Gilbert. Joker, Bombshell, Queen & Slim. Major independent co-financer. Bron Creative for TV.

Name: FilmStar Capital
Notes: Provides gap financing and equity co-financing for independent films. Works with international presales.

Name: Limelight Capital
Notes: Provides production financing including gap lending and tax credit lending for independent films.

Name: Foresight Unlimited
Notes: International sales and co-financing. Genre and commercial independent films. Festival market presence.

Name: Arclight Films
Notes: International sales and production. Gary Hamilton. Genre films and mid-budget commercial. Australia-based with global sales.

Name: Screen Media Ventures
Notes: Independent film distribution and acquisition. Part of Chicken Soup for the Soul Entertainment. Acquires completed films.

Name: Media Finance Capital
Notes: Provides production finance, gap finance, and tax credit lending for UK and European film productions.

Name: Endgame Entertainment
Notes: James D. Stern. Provides equity financing and produces. End of the Tour, Won't You Be My Neighbor?

Name: GreenBridge Films
Notes: Independent film financing company. Co-finances independent features. Works with international sales companies.

Name: Bleecker Street
Notes: Founded by Andrew Karpen. Trumbo, Captain Fantastic, Colette. Acquires and distributes quality independent films.

Name: Roadside Attractions
Notes: Eric d'Arbeloff and Howard Cohen. Manchester by the Sea, The Peanut Butter Falcon. Leading indie distributor.

Name: Focus Features
Notes: Universal Pictures' specialty label. Brokeback Mountain, Promising Young Woman, Tár. Acquires and produces prestige films.

Name: Searchlight Pictures
Notes: Disney/20th Century specialty label. The Shape of Water, Nomadland, The Banshees of Inisherin. Acquires and produces.

Name: Sony Pictures Classics
Notes: Michael Barker and Tom Bernard. Whiplash, Call Me by Your Name. Long-running specialty distributor. Festival acquisitions.

Name: Entertainment One (eOne)
Notes: Hasbro subsidiary. Film, TV, and music. The Lego Movie producer partner, Peppa Pig. Global distribution and production.

Name: STXinternational
Notes: International arm of STX. Handles overseas co-financing and distribution for STX films.

Name: Black Label Media
Notes: Trent Luckinbill, Thad Luckinbill, and Molly Smith. Sicario, La La Land (co-financed), Wind River. Active equity co-financer.

Name: Emjag Productions
Notes: Jada Pinkett Smith's production company. Focus on diverse and female-driven content.

Name: Fenix Studios
Notes: Latino-focused content studio. Produces and finances films and TV for Hispanic/Latino audiences.

Name: Picturestart
Notes: Erik Feig's company (former Lionsgate exec). Focus on YA, female-driven, and diverse content. The Secret Life of Bees. Co-finances.

Name: Makeready
Notes: Brad Weston's entertainment company. Backed by AT&T/WarnerMedia initially. The Call of the Wild. Independent film/TV production and financing.

Name: FilmRise
Notes: Danny Fisher and Jack Fisher. Acquires indie films and classic TV. Growing AVOD/SVOD distribution. Library approach.

Name: Briarcliff Entertainment
Notes: Tom Ortenberg's distribution company. Specializes in wide-release independent films.

Name: Cinereach
Notes: Nonprofit film fund. Beasts of the Southern Wild, American Honey. Provides grants and equity investment for independent and documentary film.

Name: Sundance Institute Feature Film Program
Notes: Labs, fellowships, and grants for independent narrative filmmakers. Screenwriters Lab, Directors Lab. Application-based.

Name: Tribeca Film Institute
Notes: Grants and mentorship for filmmakers. TFI Documentary Fund, Through Her Lens (women filmmakers). Application-based.

Name: National Endowment for the Arts (NEA Film)
Notes: Federal grants for media arts including film. Supports production, post-production, and distribution of film/video/audio.

Name: Firelight Media
Notes: Founded by Stanley Nelson. Documentary Lab supports emerging BIPOC documentary filmmakers. Mentorship and grants.

Name: Gucci Tribeca Documentary Fund
Notes: Tribeca Film Institute grant sponsored by Gucci supporting feature-length documentary films.

Name: Screen Australia
Notes: Australia's federal screen funding body. Production investment, development funding, enterprise funding. Also administers Producer Offset.

Name: Telefilm Canada
Notes: Canada's federal film funding body. Finances Canadian feature films. Also administers Canada Media Fund. Talent to Watch program.

Name: Screen Ireland (Fís Éireann)
Notes: Ireland's development agency for the Irish film and TV industry. Production funding, development loans. Also oversees Section 481.

Name: Swedish Film Institute
Notes: Sweden's national film funding body. Production support, development, distribution funding for Swedish films.

Name: Netherlands Film Fund
Notes: Supports Dutch film production with development, production, and distribution funding.

Name: CJ ENM (Korea)
Notes: Major Korean entertainment conglomerate. Parasite producer (via subsidiary). Acquired majority of Endeavor Content. Global content ambition.

Name: Huanxi Media
Notes: Chinese film investment and distribution company. Co-finances Chinese and international films.

Name: Village Roadshow
Notes: Major Australian entertainment company. Co-financed The Matrix, Joker, Mad Max: Fury Road with Warner Bros. Cinemas and theme parks.

Name: Creative Scotland
Notes: Scotland's national arts and screen funding body. Screen funding for Scottish film projects.

Name: Eurimages (Council of Europe)
Notes: Council of Europe film fund. Co-production support for European films. 40 member states. Application-based.

Name: MEDIA Programme (Creative Europe)
Notes: EU support programme for film and audiovisual sector. Development, distribution, audience development, festivals funding.

Name: Nordisk Film
Notes: Egmont Group subsidiary. Oldest film company in the world (1906). Scandinavian film production, distribution, and cinemas.

Name: Zentropa
Notes: Lars von Trier co-founded. Major Scandinavian production company. Dogme 95 movement. International co-productions.

Name: Bavaria Film
Notes: Major German film and TV studio. Production facilities and co-production. German market leader.

Name: Gaumont
Notes: Oldest film company in the world (1895). Major French studio. Produces and distributes film and TV globally. Lupin (Netflix).

Name: Constantin Film
Notes: Major German film production and distribution. Resident Evil franchise, Downfall. Also international co-productions.

Name: Luma Foundation
Notes: Maja Hoffmann's foundation. Supports art, documentary, and cultural projects. Arles-based cultural institution.

Name: Bertha Foundation (Documentary)
Notes: Supports documentary filmmakers and activists. Bertha DocHouse. Social justice documentary focus.

Name: Cinemaker (Film Finance)
Notes: Completion bond and gap financing specialist. Works with independent film productions needing cash-flow solutions.

Name: Film Finances Inc
Notes: World's largest completion bond company. Guarantees film delivery to financiers. Essential part of independent film finance chain.

Name: Media Capital Group
Notes: Middle East-based media investment group. Invests in international film and content productions.

Name: Image Nation Abu Dhabi
Notes: Abu Dhabi government-backed. Co-financed The Help, Contagion. Major Middle Eastern content investor. twofour54 media zone.

Name: MBC Studios
Notes: Middle East Broadcasting Center's production arm. Arabic-language and international content. Shahid streaming platform.

Name: Participant (Skoll Foundation)
Notes: Jeff Skoll's social impact media company (listed separately from Participant Media entry above as investment arm). Social impact campaigns accompany each film.
